description: Hand-curated scoring metadata for known real-world skills. Merged into inferred SkillEntry data after heuristic tag inference runs. Users can override by creating ~/.claude/skillpro/enrichment.json — the user file FULLY REPLACES this one, it does not merge.

xlsx.notes: Spreadsheet manipulation. Useful for any project that ingests/exports tabular data.

docx.notes: Word document processing. Fires for any project that generates formal documents.

pdf.notes: PDF generation/parsing. Broad applicability — don't over-tag.

pptx.notes: PowerPoint generation. Rare outside business/reporting tools.

frontend-design.notes: Production-grade frontend interfaces. Essential for any UI work.

brand-guidelines.notes: Corporate brand consistency. Applies wherever a UI is shipped.

canvas-design.notes: Visual art generation. Optional unless project needs custom graphics.

mcp-builder.notes: MCP server implementation guidance. Language-agnostic (Python + TypeScript).

claude-api.notes: Claude API integration patterns. Useful for any app that calls LLM APIs.

webapp-testing.notes: Playwright-based web app testing. Not needed for pure backend/CLI.

web-artifacts-builder.notes: HTML artifacts for Claude.ai. Web-frontend focused.

algorithmic-art.notes: p5.js generative art. Niche — optional for most projects.

skill-creator.notes: Meta-skill for authoring new skills. Optional for everyone.

slack-gif-creator.notes: Very specific niche. Not recommended by default.

agent-browser.notes: Browser automation for agents. Useful for web projects that need scraping/testing.

bmad-code-review.notes: Adversarial code review with parallel layers (Blind Hunter, Edge Case Hunter, Acceptance Auditor). Universal value across all code-producing project types.

bmad-review-edge-case-hunter.notes: Exhaustive edge case analysis of code, specs, and diffs. Method-driven boundary walking. Pairs well with testing-qa for comprehensive coverage.

bmad-review-adversarial-general.notes: Cynical review producing findings reports. Useful for any critical review session — code, design, specs, plans.

bmad-qa-generate-e2e-tests.notes: Generates end-to-end automated tests for existing features. Needs a UI or API surface to test; CLI tools and libraries get marginal value.

bmad-quick-dev.notes: General-purpose code-writing skill. Too broad to recommend by default — heuristic keeps it in optional unless the user explicitly asks.

bmad-create-architecture.notes: Technical architecture design. High-value for projects with real backend complexity; trivial for pure frontend or CLI tools.

bmad-create-prd.notes: Create a product requirements document. Pre-dev phase — no characteristic-based signal for project match. User invokes explicitly during planning.

bmad-edit-prd.notes: Edit an existing PRD. Same pre-dev rationale as bmad-create-prd.

bmad-brainstorming.notes: Interactive brainstorming sessions. Meta-skill, user-invoked. No automatic recommendation.

bmad-help.notes: BMAD navigation helper. Only useful inside a BMAD methodology workflow — not generally applicable.

bmad-distillator.notes: Lossless document compression. Niche utility — user-invoked on specific documents.

bmad-editorial-review-prose.notes: Copy-editor for prose communication issues. Most applicable to library/docs projects that ship extensive documentation.

bmad-editorial-review-structure.notes: Structural editor for reorganization and cuts. Same prose rationale.

tanstack-query-patterns.notes: Example library-anchored skill. Only surfaces when @tanstack/react-query is actually in package.json. Remove this placeholder once the real source skill lands in the federated index.

fastapi-patterns.notes: Example library-anchored skill. Paired with the fastapi detection in detect.sh.

flutter-bloc-patterns.notes: Example library-anchored skill. Fires when either the flutter_bloc or plain bloc package is detected in pubspec.yaml.

brainstorming.notes: Universal meta-skill for turning ideas into designs. Applies to every project.

writing-plans.notes: Universal meta-skill for converting specs into implementation plans.

executing-plans.notes: Universal meta-skill — the execution counterpart to writing-plans.

debugging.notes: Universal root-cause-analysis skill. Every project needs debugging patterns.

refactoring.notes: Universal refactoring discipline. Applies to any codebase.

writing-tests.notes: Universal test-writing discipline. Every project benefits.

using-git-worktrees.notes: Isolated workspace pattern for any git-based project.

subagent-driven-development.notes: Parallel agent workflow. Universal applicability for complex work.

receiving-code-review.notes: How to act on code review feedback. Universal.

testing-anti-patterns.notes: What NOT to do in tests. Universal.

condition-based-waiting.notes: Pattern for avoiding race conditions in async testing/scripts.

creating-skills.notes: How to author a skill. Relevant to anyone using Claude Code.
